concepts.finding: Preserves first-order proximity (observed links) and second-order proximity (shared neighborhood). Second-order proximity captures that nodes with shared neighbors should be similar even with no direct edge. Edge-sampling algorithm addresses gradient explosion with weighted edges. Scales to millions of nodes on single machine.
concepts.implication: Second-order proximity in keyword graphs captures that two keywords are similar if they co-occur with similar neighbor keywords - even if they never directly co-occur. This is critical for synonyms and paraphrases.
concepts.open_question: What is the relative importance of first-order vs second-order proximity for keyword intent clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Second-order proximity (LINE 2nd order) better captures synonym intent relationships than first-order proximity for keyword embeddings.
